args.existingTab: true or explicit policy to claim a user-open Chrome tab instead of opening a replacement
args.prompt: message to send or workflow-specific input

examples: await chatgpt.ask({ prompt: "reply with the word hi", wait: true, read: true });
result.name: ask
result.retryPolicy: Return structured CommandResult failures; do not resubmit prompts unless the sequence policy permits unmatched-turn recovery.
result.summary: Ask ChatGPT in a new or selected thread, optionally with files, wait/read, downloads, and reports.

args.existingTab: true or explicit policy to claim a user-open Chrome tab instead of opening a replacement
args.prompt: message to send or workflow-specific input

examples: await chatgpt.askInThread({ thread: { type: "search", query: "Naming macOS Utility" }, prompt: "Continue." });
await chatgpt.askInThread({ thread: { type: "url", url: "https://chatgpt.com/c/<conversation-id>" }, existingTab: true, prompt: "Continue." });
result.name: askInThread
result.retryPolicy: Return structured CommandResult failures; do not resubmit prompts unless the sequence policy permits unmatched-turn recovery.
result.summary: Open or claim an existing thread by URL, conversation id, title, or search query, then ask and read.

args.existingTab: true or explicit policy to claim a user-open Chrome tab instead of opening a replacement
args.files: absolute local file paths to attach before submitting
args.mode: optional visible mode selection, e.g. { effort: "Thinking" }
args.prompt: message to send after files are attached
args.read: true or read options such as { format: "markdown" }; defaults to Markdown
args.wait: true or wait options; defaults to true

examples: await chatgpt.askWithFiles({ thread: { type: "url", url: "https://chatgpt.com/c/<conversation-id>" }, existingTab: true, mode: { effort: "Thinking" }, files: ["/absolute/host/path/brief.md"], prompt: "Summarize this.", wait: true, read: { format: "markdown" } });
result.name: askWithFiles
result.retryPolicy: Return structured CommandResult failures; do not resubmit prompts unless the sequence policy permits unmatched-turn recovery.
result.summary: Attach absolute local file paths, optionally set mode, ask, wait, and read.

args.existingTab: true or explicit policy to claim a user-open Chrome tab instead of opening a replacement
args.prompt: message to send or workflow-specific input

examples: await chatgpt.askAndDownload({ prompt: "Create a CSV.", download: { destDir: "/absolute/host/output" }, wait: true });

result.retryPolicy: Return structured CommandResult failures; do not resubmit prompts unless the sequence policy permits unmatched-turn recovery.
result.summary: Ask ChatGPT to produce a visible downloadable output and save the latest exposed file.

args.existingTab: true or explicit policy to claim a user-open Chrome tab instead of opening a replacement

examples: const agent = chatgpt.agent({ name: "reviewer", instructions: "Review deeply." }); await chatgpt.runner.run(agent, { input: "Review this.", thread: { type: "new" } });
await chatgpt.runner.run(agent, { input: "Continue.", thread: { type: "url", url: "https://chatgpt.com/c/<conversation-id>" }, existingTab: true });

examples: await chatgpt.runPlan({ name: "doctor-upload" });
result.name: doctor-upload
result.retryPolicy: Return structured readiness checks; retry only after the reported blocker or permission setting changes.
result.summary: Named macro: preflight bridge, login, and upload permission remediation.

examples: await chatgpt.runPlan({ name: "redacted-run-report", input: { result } });
result.name: redacted-run-report
result.retryPolicy: Do not retry blindly; preserve redaction defaults and report filesystem errors as CommandResult failures.
result.summary: Named macro: create a redacted report for a supplied CommandResult.

args.existingTab: optional exact existing-tab policy for check: ["existing_tab"]
args.files: optional file paths for the Stage 2 file_preflight scaffold
args.report: optional report output policy for check: ["reports"]

examples: await chatgpt.doctor({ check: ["bridge", "login", "upload"] });
await chatgpt.doctor({ check: ["existing_tab"], existingTab: { target: { type: "conversationId", conversationId: "<conversation-id>" }, ifMissing: "block" } });
await chatgpt.doctor({ check: ["localization", "reports"], report: { destDir: "/absolute/host/reports" } });
result.name: doctor
result.retryPolicy: Return structured readiness checks; retry only after the reported blocker or permission setting changes.
result.summary: Preflight browser bridge, login, upload, existing-tab, artifact, localization, report, and selector readiness.

examples: await chatgpt.createReport(result, { destDir: "/absolute/host/reports" });
result.name: createReport
result.retryPolicy: Do not retry blindly; preserve redaction defaults and report filesystem errors as CommandResult failures.
result.summary: Write a durable redacted run report for a command result.
